extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam won the sprint after Jumbo-Visma paced the final climb, taking bonus seconds while KÜNG retained yellow before mountain stages.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled final classified climb despite having no sprinter, revealing GC defense strategy rather than sprint setup.
Breakaway ZUKOWSKI prioritized KOM points over stage win, collecting points on all three climbs for jersey calculation.
EVENEPOEL contested tiso kilometer bonus seconds despite flat sprint stage, signaling early GC time-gathering aggression.
BALDE (DSM) leadout work 300m from line indicates team prioritized positioning over power for sprint delivery.

rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: First win since February crash during classics shows form recovery timing perfectly for Tour debut sprint stages.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Third place suggests decent form but losing sprint to Girmay may indicate incomplete recovery or tactics.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Actively hunting tiso kilometer bonus seconds on flat stage reveals opportunistic time-gathering approach before mountains.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Fifth after altitude block on final Tour de Suisse suggests reasonable sharpness but not peak sprint form.
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: TT winner defending yellow passively before mountains suggests team knows lead temporary, conserving energy.
